WordPress Security Best Practices

Posted on January 15, 2024

WordPress powers over 40% of the web. With such widespread adoption, security is paramount. Here are essential practices for keeping your WordPress site safe.

Keep Everything Updated

The first and most important step is keeping WordPress core, themes, and plugins updated. Security updates patch known vulnerabilities.

Use Strong Passwords

Never use simple passwords like "admin123". Use a password manager and create unique, complex passwords for all admin accounts.

Limit Login Attempts

Install a plugin that limits failed login attempts. Brute force attacks are common but can be defeated with rate limiting.
